feed.title: Accessibility Wars - voe
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: I'll Never Yield [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This raid build focuses on utilizing Warrior's strong Dual Wielding trait on Paragon, allowing for a selfless low intensity DPS that's great for group content.

It requires the Weaponmaster Training from Secrets of the Obscure to be able to wield the dagger as Paragon.

It also has high burst APM, as there is a 5 button combo that you ideally press quickly. Expect lower DPS if you do not press this combo quickly.

Consider it a more selfless alternative to the Magebane Daggerbreaker build.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Berserker's Force
Mace Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Claw
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Standard Build

[&DQIEHyQ2SidwAHAAdh12Ha8ArwCpAKkA7gDuAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADLwA1AAUAAA==]

Rotation

At the start of the fight, use all of the signets you have, saving Signet of Fury for right when you are about to attack the boss.

  1. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  2. Signet of Fury (won't be available in opener)
  3. Chant of Action (F2) (only if it is not active or your motivation is less than 7)
  4. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  5. Tremor (Mace 5)
  6. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  7. Auto until F1 is up again
  8. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  9. Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3)
  10. Auto until Mace 5 has less than 2 seconds left on its cooldown

Notes

Relic

Upkeep Relic of the Claw with Tremor (Mace 5) and Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3). The rotation is made in such a way that you are reaching ~90% uptime of Claw without much thought. For slightly higher Claw uptime, delay your Dagger 3 a little bit instead of doing it immediately after your Dagger F1.

You can also use Relic of the Eagle for a DPS loss of ~3k.

Weapon Set 2 options

Dagger / Axe for more DPS but higher APM

Your rotation becomes:

  1. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  2. Signet of Fury (won't be available in opener)
  3. Chant of Action (F2) (if it is not active or your motivation is less than 7)
  4. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  5. Tremor (Mace 5)
  6. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  7. Weapon swap
  8. Dual Strike (Axe 4)
  9. Whirling Axe (Axe 5)
  10. Auto until F1 is up again
  11. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  12. Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3)
  13. Weapon swap
  14. Auto until Mace 5 has 2 seconds left on its cooldown
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Berserker's Force
Axe Berserker's Hydromancy

Remember you don't need to buy a second Dagger; just equip the Axe offhand.

Spear for CC

When you need to do big CC, swap to Spear and use skills 3 and 5. Swap back once CC is done.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Berserker's Hydromancy
Paralyzation

Chants

Your F2's refrain is what you will be camping most of the time. However, don't forget your other chants.

Most notably, Chant of Freedom is a group stun break and also gives stability. To use it, simply press it, then immediately use your F2 again; it's a 15% DPS loss to forget to head back to your F2 chant, so make sure to do so.

Utilities and Elites

Signet of Fury is the only utility skill truly required for this build, as removing all of the other Signets results in only a 3k DPS loss. When the build already is getting ~35k, this is more than acceptable if you're willing to bring utility for your team. Discuss with your allies if there's anything that needs covered, such as CC, Resistance, Stability, etc.

As far as what to replace, always keep your Signet of Fury as it's a big DPS loss to remove it. Removing Signet of Might is also a DPS loss, but if your team needs extra help it's still worth taking off.

Elites

For your elites, you have two options:

Would you like to resurrect downed people, up to five, almost instantly? Battle Standard is an easy choice and is the usual default; it's hard to wrong here.

Would you like to prevent mistakes from ever happening to begin with, or ignore mechanics that would otherwise down the whole group? Feel confident in timing it correctly? "We Will Never Yield!" is your new best friend. Some places you can bring it include:

  • Sunqua Peak CM (for all of the spread mechanics)
  • Conjured Amalgamate (for the clap)
  • Cardinal Sabir (ignoring tornadoes / "uppies" and "corners")
  • Deimos CM (for a guaranteed chill Mind Crush in case Aegis gets stripped, such as if oils were spread at the same time as Mind Crush)

Utilities

Consider for your utilities:

  • Throw Bolas for long-range immobilization.
  • Kick, Bull's Charge, "On Your Knees!" and Banner of Strength for more CC.
  • Banner of Tactics for superspeed, stability, resistance and group stun break.
  • "Never Surrender!" for resistance, aegis, resolution, and group stun break.
  • Banner of Defense for barrier or aegis.
  • "Brace Yourselves!" for barrier.
  • Endure Pain or Defiant Stance for ignoring damage, allowing you to skip mechanics like Deimos's Mind Crush ("Stand in the ward!").

Video

Changes

content/builds/power-paragon.md was changed:

  • Hushblade: Swipe out in a cone before you, dazing enemies struck.

entry.title: Gambit [aDPS]
entry.content:
The purpose of writing this guide is to provide a competent DPS options with good alacrity uptime without forced movement or weapon swaps. The base attack is quite high and fumbling with your skills is not severely punishing.

The alternative weapon sets (rifle and dagger) are not covered in this guide. The concept for the other weapons is relatively simple and you may find more information on the build guides of other sites such as SnowCrows. This build has intermediate difficulty and although the APM is relatively high (70), most of the button presses are attributed to a single skill, Weakening Whirl (Staff 2), which simplifies the rotation a lot. The rotation can be simplified (reduction of ~ 20 APM) by holding down Staff 2 for continuous casts , while other skills only require around 30 APM.

This build heavily relies on Skritt Scuffle for alacrity. Moving out of the scuffle radius (360) will result in missing alacrity for a significant amount of time.

Gearing and Consumables

GW2Skills Links

  1. Staff Loadout

Equipment

  • Full Berserker's
ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Diviner's Concentration
Force
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Thief
  • Compared to other build guides, addition concentration is added to this guide to allow easier alacrity uptime. You may add even more Diviner's pieces with minimal loss of DPS.

Weapon Swap Options

Using dagger/rifle with Metal Legion Guitar will result in a moderate DPS increase and better initiative management.

Food and Utility

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

Alac DPS (Optimised)

[&DQUjGxw9TS8MAQAAWR0AAGAdAAAzAQAAfB0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWQA2AAA=]

Rotation and Utilities

  1. Use Stone Summit Cannon and its backfire off-cooldown
  2. Use Skritt Swipe and Skritt Scuffle off-cooldown
    • use your stolen skills charges except for Metal Legion Guitar for alacrity
    • try to catch different stolen skills, you may prioritise skills that provide power/strike damage/initiative
    • use Metal Legion Guitar with Backstab if weapon swap is desired (optional, see SC guide)
  3. Use all your initiative on Weakening Whirl (Staff 2)
  4. Use Canach-Coin Toss if you have nothing better to do or replace it with another utility, do not backfire this.
  • Use Skritt Scuffle when Skritt Swipe is recharging
  • Avoid Skritt Swipe when Skritt Scuffle is active

CC options

  • Metal Legion Guitar
  • Hook Strike (stealth/guitar)
  • Helmet Breaker
  • Hook strike is a powerful CC skill that does not require initiative to cast. This is especially efficient when Metal Legion Guitar is used, giving three charges without the need for stealth.

Sustainability for Solo/OW

This build has a lot of sustainability through its baseline traits Magpie's Defense and Enterprising Aristocrat.

You may equip the following for extra:

  •  Relic of Zakiros
  • Emergency Jade Shield
  • Invigorating Precision

Benchmark

The benchmark can vary greatly depending on which stolen skills you get. The numbers are not grinded for and it is likely you will achieve higher DPS than our attempts.

entry.title: 0-KIT ALAC-T1002-P [aDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build has no kits and provides reliable DPS and Alacrity. The kits can be added back for additional DPS when one is comfortable with the build.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Diviner's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Diviner's
Ring Diviner's
Relic Claw
  • Start by adding Diviner's gear until your boon duration is about 20-25%. Fill the rest with Dragon's and Berserker's gear.
  • Be careful not to overcap crit due to High Caliber.

Other weapons

Shield Berserker's Air

This can be used if more CC is desired for a small DPS loss.

Food and Utility and Infusions

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMmHwY7SyoqD4QAjgEmASYBAABaHQAAex2JAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgA2AFUAAA==]

GW2Skills Link

Rotation

Granting Alacrity

You give Alacrity with Morph Skills:

  1. Offensive Protocol: Obliterate
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce
  3. Defensive Protocol: Thorns
  • You should use these skills spread out but frequently enough to ensure Alacrity doesn't drop.
  • Offensive Protocol: Pierce is important to upkeep  Relic of the Claw

DPS Rotation

  1. Open with Rocket Turret and DO NOT Detonate Rocket Turret
  2. Use all your skills off-cooldown
  3. Spread out morph skills to maintain Willing Host
  4. Spread out CC skills to maintain  Relic of the Claw

CC skills

  1. Explosive Rockets (used in opener)
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce (used in DPS rotation)
  3. Throw Mine (used in DPS rotation)
  4. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt - backup)

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: Sword Saint Commander [aHeal]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build which uses Celestial gear and hybrid traits to maximise the sword's physical prowress and bleeding capabilities. This build is designed to replace a mod-high toughness dedicated healer slot. Compared to other celestial healers, this build has one of the lower damage ceilings and is less preferred in the meta. However, it is favored on this site due to the easily achievable high damage floor (14k Sw/WH camp).

Hybrid healing builds have a lot of give and take and toe the line to bring 'just enough', so you need to adjust the build to the strengths of your team composition.

Gearing

EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Hvy ArmorCelestial Superior Rune of Divinity
Sword (MH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Torment
Sword (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
All TrinketsCelestialN/A
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Fractal
Relic (Heal)N/ARelic of the Flock
Relic (Sust)N/A-

Optional weapon sets

EquipmentStatSigil
Warhorn (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Mace (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Dagger (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Shield (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Paralyzation

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Spherified Cilantro Oyster Soup
  • Enhancement:  Magnanimous Tuning Crystal
  • It is possible to mix other stats to increase DPS but you want to at least maintain 1000 healing power and 40% boon duration.
  • Try to maintain at least 10k DPS or you may opt to do the reverberation healing build instead.
  • If you ever find yourself switching to staff, play a dedicated healer instead.
  • Food and utilities with universal stats do very well
    1.  Decade Enhancement Station
    2.  Snow Diamond Ornament
    3. Fractal Cooking/Sharpening Golem

Build

DPS Group Content

[&DQIkNQsuShZ5HQAAqAAAAK0BAAB+HQAA7gAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]
  • Blademaster provides a higher DPS but Furious is better if you are sitting on Warhorn for long periods.
  • Reverberation is a very powerful flexible support trait with minimal DPS loss.

Rotation

Opener

Your ability to perform the opener will depend on the boss and you may need to swap the order of the skills.

  1. Commands
    • "Brace Yourselves!" for adrenaline
    • "Never Surrender!" for boons
  2. Chant Skills
    • Chant of Action
    • Chant of Recuperation
    • Chant of Freedom
  3. Use warhorn skills
    • Charge
    • Call of Valor
  4. Weapon swap ⚔ for DPS
  • Your Chant skills provide alacrity and boons, you need to keep swapping between them to reduce their own cooldowns and maintain boons.
  • Periodically swap to warhorns for extra barrier and swiftness.

DPS rotation

Use the following off-cooldown

  1. Bloodthirster (⚔ Burst)
  2. Impale (⚔ 4 Optional)
  3. Rend (⚔ 3)
  • If you need CC, using mace in the offhand shortens your DPS rotation with only minimal loss in DPS.
  • While swapped to warhorn, Savage Leap may result in a DPS increase.

General House Rules

  1. Your primary goal is to maintain boons, alacrity and your party's survivability before DPS
  2. You need to maintain 8-10 boons and 100% alacrity uptime.

Crowd Control

Reverberation and "On Your Knees!" provides a solid AOE defiance break, (eg. shields on CA) such that you will rarely require anything else. Additional options are listed here for posterity:

  • You may carry off-hand mace, dagger or shield for additional hard CC.
  • Take off-hand dagger if you require a boon strip.
  • Other utilities with defiance breaks may be picked with caution.

Latest patch changes

  • The new changes to dagger allows it to be used for both boon-strip and CC.
  • Sword no longer scales more towards condi but the build still retains its high damage floor.

entry.title: Holy Nova [aHeal]
entry.content:
This is a very accessible build that relies on an excessive amount of healing, CC, condi cleanses and utilites, more than you will ever need. Ideally, the build should function as a low-toughness healer.

This build is low cognition, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content. Although the maximum APM of the build is quite high, you probably can survive without mashing keys due to how much healing is baked into the core guardian and Luminary kit. This build requires only one weapon with no requirement of a weapon swap although you must interact with the Luminary's Forge ability.

There is an excessive amount of light effects on this build and you may wish to adjust your graphics settings if the intensity is too high.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Harrier's Transference
Concentration
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Karakosa

Alternate Pieces

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Harrier's Transference
Concentration
  •  Relic of the Flock
  •  Relic of Febe

Note: Weapon swap is optional. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements. Note: Febe gives patchy swiftness coverage but allows you to avoid swapping to staff for Symbol of Swiftness. Note: If you do not use these sources of swifness, you will need to take "Advance!".

Budget Options

  • Replace  Superior Rune of the Monk with  Superior Rune of Rebirth
  • Replace  Superior Sigil of Transference with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Bountiful Maintenance Oil

Build

Heal Alacrity Build

[&DQExLS45URVhHTgBVx0tAXgdSAFWHTIBiRIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACMwAJAQA=]

Trait Alternatives

  • Bring Master of Consecrations if you are taking consecrations.
  • This build is a simplified version of the heal alacrity build on snowcrows with selective preferences for lower intensity. If you are able to adapt to the version on SC, feel free to follow their guide.

Rotation and Utilities

The excessive amount of healing provided by this build piles on top of the already flexible healing and support capabilties of the Core Guardian kit. Of note, Light's Gift causes Luminary's Blessing to heal allies whenever it is refreshed or expires.

You should not be adjusting the build to try to increase healing but focus on your utilities instead.

Heal and Alacrity Alac Rotation

  1. Use your virtues before Enter Radiant Forge
  2. Use skills 5-4-3-2 in Forge
    • to grant alacrity
    • for a strong CC
    • To burst heal
  3. Exit Radiant Forge
  4. Using any of your utilities will overcap on protection, which can be used on cooldown or reserved
    • Reserve Resolute Stance for Condi Cleanse and healing
    • Reserve Stalwart Stance for Aegis
    • Reserve Valorous Stance for Stability
    • Reserve Effulgent Stance for extra CC
    • Use "Feel My Wrath!" on cooldown for extra Fury uptime
  5. Use skills 5-4-3-2 with your spear
    • To grant Fury
    • For a minor CC
    • To burst heal
  6. Repeat step 1 when refreshed.
  • Note: If your might uptime is patchy, consider spamming Radiant Justice (F1) during your Forge rotation.

Crowd Control rotation

  1. You have decent CC using only Brilliant Slam (Forge 5 Flip)
  2. You can extend your CC rotation by starting with Dazzling Hammer (Forge 2) i.e. 2-5-4-3-2
  3. Use Effulgent Stance for extra CC
  4. Symbol of Luminance (Spear 5)
  5. Sanctuary (Alt Utility Skill)
  • Note: More options are available but not exhausted in this guide.

Condi Cleanses

  1. Radiant Resolve (F2)
  2. Restorative Glow (Forge 3 Flip)
  3. Resolute Stance (Heal)
  4. Solar Storm (Spear 4)
  5. Purging Flames (Alt Utility)
  6. Smite Condition (Alt Utility)
  • Note: It is very unlikely that you will require to use alternate utilities for extra condi cleanses.

Situational Utilties/Relics

  1. Wall of Reflection
  2. Hallowed Ground
  3. "Stand Your Ground!"
  4. "Advance!" (Swiftness)
  • Note: If you do not use Symbol of Swiftness (Staff 3) or  Relic of Febe, you will need alternate sources of swiftness like "Advance!".
  • Note: "Advance!" has to be used on cooldown for 100% swiftness coverage but you can rely on Radiant Courage as an alternate source of Aegis.
  • Note: Symbol of Swiftness now grants superspeed on its first pulse.

entry.title: Rifle and Roses [qHeal]
entry.content:
The Troubadour has an easy design such that when you randomly mash all the buttons, you'd provide all the right boons and an excessive amount of healing. This build has selected utilities for minimal cognitive complexity, such that the hardest part of playing this build is enduring the out-of-sync music throughout the fight.

While this guide assumes no weapon swap is needed, if you take an off-tank role, you may consider swapping rifle to other alternatives.

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Rifle Harrier's Paralyzation
Transference
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Febe

This is a low toughness set-up.

Alternative

Weapon swap is optional:

Scepter and shield for tanking

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Harrier's Transference
Shield Harrier's Paralyzation

Scepter and focus for additional CC/pulls.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Harrier's Transference
Focus Minstrel's Renewal

Shield is better used for tanking and focus is useful for pulling adds. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

 

Budget Options

  • Replace any of the sigil with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Magnanimous Maintenance Oil OR  Holographic Super Drumstick (budget alternative)

Build

Heal Quickness Build

[&DQctOhcqSTptAQ8BUx2GAYQdgQFrHWsBbR28AQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABWQAA]

Rotation and Utilities

There is no fixed rotation for this build. Even if you randomly press buttons you will still provide boons and adequate heals.

Quickness

You will need to use the following for quickness:

  1. Lively Lute
  2. Crescendo (while as many instruments are playing as possible)

Swiftness

  1. Mantra of Recovery
  2. Power Return

Your healing skills trigger  Relic of Febe to grant swiftness, use them off cooldown.

Shield or Focus can partially cover quickness, allowing you to drop  Relic of Febe

General rotation

  1. Generate Notes (Clones)
    • Journey (R2)
    • Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
    • Mantra of Recovery -> Power Return (Heal)
    • Any tale with instrument-pairing
  2. Spend Notes to play Instruments as soon as they recharged (2 or more notes)
  3. Use Crescendo when you have 3 or more instruments playing
  4. You provide lots of boons and healing through instruments, rifle skills and utilities provide even more of them.

Utilty

You may replace skill slot 9 with any of the following:

  1. Tale of the Valiant Marshal for a stun break + short stability + additional notes
  2. Mantra of Concentration for permanent stability
  3. Mantra of Resolve for heavy condi cleanses
  4. Feedback for reflects
  5. Portal Entre for skips

Crowd Control

You have decent CC with your base kit:

  1. Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
  2. Deafening Drum (F3)
  3. Tale of the August Queen (Elite)
  4. Crescendo (while Drum is playing)

Your weapon swaps allow access to additional CCs.

Optimisations

Mesmer as a class is designed to be flexible. The skill choices on this site are designed to be simple. There are an incredible amount of permutations that are not exhausted in this guide. You may introduce additional utilities when you are comfortable so play around.

entry.title: 0-KIT ALAC-T1000-C [aDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build is equipped similar to the Snowcrow's guide but reduces the need for kits. The kits can be added back for additional DPS when one is comfortable with the build.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Celestial Bursting
Earth
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Celestial
Amulet Sinister
Accessory Celestial
Accessory Celestial
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Viper's
Ring Viper's
Relic Fractal
  • Start by adding Viper/Celestial Pieces until your condi duration is about 67%. More celestial pieces will provide more concentration and boon duration of 12-30% is ideal. You can fill the remaining slots with Sinister or Grieving gear. The above gear has 16% boon duration.

Food and Utility and Infusions

  •  Cilantro and Cured Meat Flatbread
  •  Toxic Tuning Crystal
  •  Malign +5 Agony Infusion (optional)

Build

[&DQMGJyY5SyYqDwAAWh0AAIIdAAAiAQAAex0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACCQE2AAA=]

GW2Skills Link

Rotation

Granting Alacrity

You give Alacrity with Morph Skills:

  1. Offensive Protocol: Obliterate
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce
  3. Defensive Protocol: Thorns
  • You may replace Offensive Protocol: Pierce with another protocol of choice for additional utility.
  • You should use these skills spread out but frequently enough to ensure Alacrity doesn't drop.
  • Offensive Protocol: Obliterate and Defensive Protocol: Thorns are your main damage buffs.

DPS Rotation

  1. Precast Flame Turret and DO NOT Detonate Flame Turret
  2. Use all your skills off-cooldown except your CC ( Static Shock & Roiling Skies) and healing skill ( Static Shock)
  3. Spread out morph skills to maintain Willing Host

CC skills

  1. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt)
  2. Roiling Skies (Spear 4)
  3. Offensive Protocol: Pierce (used in DPS rotation)
  4. Flux State (used in DPS rotation)

Sustainability

  1. Liquid State is an evade stance
  2. A.E.D. can bypass many mechanics.
  3. Stainless Steel is useful for additional heals
  4. Innervating Alloy can be used for condi cleanses
  5.  Relic of the Biomancer or Mist Stranger for Open-world

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: Power Troubadour [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Melee Power build with high burst damage, ability to skip mechanics, and option to swap out utility for much more CC.

 

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Berserker's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Assassin's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone
 

Build

[&DQcBHQoaSScjDwAAaQEAAIIBAABmHQAAbR0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAyAAA=]
 

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  2. Lively Lute
  3. Flustering Flute
  4. Deafening Drum
  5. Crescendo

Your opener is also your burst rotation, and should be used whenever Crescendo is coming off cooldown.

Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) and Lively Lute have the same cooldown, and should always be used together. This is your highest priority and should not be delayed for anything else, and it should be extended into the burst rotation when Crescendo is available.

Take note that you do want to use Lively Lute with 3 Notes active for maximum damage, but by following this rotation you will always have 3 Notes when casting Lively Lute, removing one source of resource management.

On top of this, you should:

Use off cooldown:

  1. Power Spike (Only use 1 charge)
  2. Blurred Frenzy
  3. Tale of the Tortured Mastermind

In that order of priority. Power Spike has no cast time and can be used during the cast of another skill without interrupting it.

 

Tale of the August Queen is a 2 second long immune skill, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where it's cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Illusionary Leap --> Swap
  2. Deafening Drum

and you can swap out Tale of the August Queen for Signet of Humility if a lot of CC is required for the encounter.

 

Video

Changes

content/builds/power-troubadour.md was changed:

  • Vicious Expression: This trait now increases base strike damage and is further increased against boonless foes.

entry.title: Galvanic Enchantment [DPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build from the new expansion, Visions of Eternity, and is currently overperforming. Expect it to be balanced in the near future.

This build combines the concepts used in other Elementalist Inferno builds into the new Evoker elite specialization.

Instead of taking Specialized Elements like every other mono element Evoker build, this build uses Galvanic Enchantment.

Doing so allows us to reduce the APM of the build, and allowing for easy to access and fast CC, at the cost of some DPS.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Flame Legion
Berserker's Flame Legion
Berserker's Flame Legion
Berserker's Flame Legion
Berserker's Flame Legion
Berserker's Flame Legion
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Berserker's Smoldering
Focus Berserker's Bursting
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Fireworks
  • Food:  Plate of Coq Au Vin with Salsa or  Plate of Truffle Steak
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQYfPSkfUBViHXQAjgDLAMsAcwB3HVABJgAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADVgAvADEAAA==]

Rotation

The rotation for Evoker consists mainly of charging and casting both Ignite and Conflagration as quickly and as many times as possible. Using any weapon skill 3 times will charge Ignite, and using Ignite 3 times will charge Conflagration.

Your rotation consists of a simple opener and a Fire Attunement priority.

Try to always precast 2 stacks of Ignite before the fight begins. This can be done by swapping to Splash and using it twice as Splash can be used without any target selected. Remember to swap back to Ignite once you have finished precasting.

Opener:

  1. Always be in Fire Attunement
  2. Dragon's Tooth (Scepter Fire 2)
  3. Flamewall (Focus Fire 4)
  4. Ignite
  5. Phoenix (Scepter Fire 3)
  6. Conflagration
  7. Fire Shield (Focus Fire 5)
  8. Fox's Fury
  9. Signet of Fire

Fire Attunement Priority:

  1. Conflagration
  2. Ignite
  3. Phoenix (Scepter Fire 3)
  4. Flamewall (Focus Fire 4)
  5. Dragon's Tooth (Scepter Fire 2)
  6. Fire Shield (Focus Fire 5)
  7. Fox's Fury
  8. Signet of Fire
  9. Glyph of Elementals

Basically putting your professional skill first, weapon skills next, then utilities.

Take note that casting Ignite too quickly after Conflagration may result in you cancelling Conflagration. However, this is a much more common problem with the fast pace of the Specialized Elements build, and is usually not a problem here apart from the opener.

This build relies heavily on landing Flamewall (Focus Fire 4) to maintain  Relic of Fireworks uptime. Each tick of damage from the fire field generated by Flamewall (Focus Fire 4) resets  Relic of Fireworks duration, allowing you to easily maintain 100% updtime with just 1 skill and no cooldown management.

You should also:

Cast Off Cooldown:

  1. Cleansing Fire
  2. Flame Barrage

All of these skills have no cast time and can be cast anytime.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are:

CC Skills:

  1. Shatterstone (Scepter Water 2)
  2. Freezing Gust (Focus Water 4)
  3. Comet (Focus Water 5)
  4. Blinding Flash (Scepter Air 3)
  5. Gale (Focus Air 5)
  6. Dust Devil (Scepter Earth 3)
  7. Magnetic Wave (Focus Earth 4)

To CC, perform the following:

Simplified CC Rotation:

  1. Water Attunement 2 4 5
  2. Air Attunement 3 5
  3. Earth Attunement 3 4

Then return to Fire Attunement as soon as you are able to. You can cut short the rotation and return to Fire Attunement as soon as the bar breaks instead of performing the full rotation.

Video

Build Demo

Changes

content/builds/hybrid-evoker.md was changed:

  • Inferno: Increased the power scaling from 7.5% to 8.25% in PvE only.
  • Fiery Frost: Increased the evade distance from 250 to 510. This skill now removes inhibiting conditions.
  • Glacial Drift: Increased the projectile speed. This skill now dazes enemies that it strikes.
  • Earthen Synergy: Increased the stun duration from 1.5 seconds to 2 seconds.
  • Stone Tide: The first pulse of this skill will now cripple enemies that it strikes.

entry.title: Vocals of Eternity [aHeal]
entry.content:
Notes

Its quite hard to do badly on this build... This build gambles on Reverberation to empower Commands.

Gearing

EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Hvy ArmorHarrier's Superior Rune of the Monk
Staff (2H)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Transference
-- Superior Sigil of Concentration
Dagger (MH)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Water
Warhorn (OH)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Renewal
All TrinketsMinstrel'sN/A
RelicN/A Relic of the Flock

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish
  • Enhancement:  Bountiful Maintenance Oil

Optional weapon sets

You may replace Dagger/warhorn

Main Hand

  • Mace (for Tanking and CC)

Off Hand

  • Mace (for CC)
  • Dagger (Boon Strip and CC)

Build

GW2Skills Link

[&DQILHzM/Sh55HQAAhh0AAH4dAAB2HQAAcx0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

Other than providing alacrity, there isn't a real rotation, you could mash all the buttons on cooldown and it will heal, provide 10-11 boons and bruteforce heal/barrier most mechanics.

However, the build has a lot of room for optimisation and it is important to reserve some skills for when you require them. The situations below are non-exhaustive and you will find more uses that will not be covered in this guide. However because there are many options, you will still perform well if you mess up badly or do not follow the rotation described below.

Opener

The opener is optional and helps to speed up initial ramp of adrenaline and prioritises might uptime.

  1. Use all your commands to build adrenaline.
  2. Chant of Action (F2)_
  3. Path to Victory (F1)
  4. 🔁Weapon swap (to Warhorn) and use WH skills

    Charge Call of Valor

  5. Rotate your chants to provide alacrity.
  6. 🔁Weapon swap (to Staff)

Providing Alacrity

Alternate your chants every 6s to provide alacrity. Use Valiant Leap (⚕️2) and Defiant Roar (⚕️5) to get adrenaline. For Chants, there is a certain priority depending on the situation:

SkillKeyWhen prioritised
Chant of ActionF2To give might and fury
Chant of RecuperationF3When you need heals
Chant of FreedomF4When you need stability/stun-break

Commands and Other Boons

You must use your commands to grant boons, but you must also reserve some for emergencies.

SkillEffect
"We Will Never Yield!"Used to bypass raid mechanics
"We Shall Return!"Lots of healing and condi-cleanses
"Brace Yourselves!"Barrier
"Find Their Weakness!"For Might uptime
"Never Surrender!"For Aegis, Resistance, Resolution
  • "Find Their Weakness!" and "Never Surrender!" should be used as part of your rotation to maintain boons.
  • Many of your other boons are covered by your weapons and there is some overlap.

Crowd Control

Command CC

"On Your Knees!" is a very strong pulsing CC that is ample in almost all situations. Use it to replace "Find Their Weakness!"

Weapon skills that provide breakbar damage

  1. Disrupting Stab (🗡️3)
  2. Snap Pull (⚕️4)
  3. Skull Crack (🏏F1)
  4. Tremor (🏏5)
  5. Pommel Bash (🏏3) (only when tanking)

CC Utilities
Additional CC options are available but reduce your might uptime when you switch out another command to use them. I do not recommend doing so and hence it will not be covered in the guide.

entry.title: Dual Blademaster [cDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build which uses mostly Vipers Gear to maximise the dual swords new reworked bleeding capabilities.

This build has a lot of auto attacks.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Grieving Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Sword (MH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Torment
Sword (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
All TrinketsViper'sN/A
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Fractal
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Thorn
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Biomancer
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Mistfire

Optional equipment sets

EquipmentStatSigil
Mace (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
Dagger (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
  • Note: Sinister stats can be used in place of the one Grieving piece.

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Spherified Cilantro Oyster Soup
  • Food:  Dragon's Revelry Starcake (Budget)
  • Food:  Feast of Delectable Birthday Cake (Budget)
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal
  • Better (prohibitively expensive) food/enhancements are available
    • Snow Diamond Ornament
    •  Writ of Masterful Malice
    •  Writ of Masterful Accuracy
    • Cooking Golem and/or Sharpening Golem (may be freely available on occasion)

Infusions

  •  Malign WvW Infusion can be used for all empty slots (optional)

Build

Viper's Variant

[&DQIEHyQ+SiWmAAAAswAAAK8AAACpAAAAnAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

Opener

  1. Signets (Cast very early to maintain 5 stacks)
  2. Chant of Action
  3. "To the Limit!"
  • None of these skills will trigger the boss fight, and you can perpetually keep refreshing signet until the fight begins.
  • Signet of Fury and "To the Limit!" provide adrenaline so you may use them in between fight phases or as part of your opener

Skill Priority

Use the following off-cooldown

  1. Bloodthirster (⚔ Burst)
  2. Impale (⚔ 4)
  3. Rend (⚔ 3)
  4. Dolyak Signet
  5. Signet of Might (if required)
  6. Other Signets only if required
  • Try to maintain 4-5 stacks of Signet Mastery.
  • Dolyak Signet has no cast time.
  • Signet of Might has no cast time.

Crowd Control

This build has very poor CC options and you may bring additional CC by sacrificing Dolyak Signet but Signet Mastery will be harder to maintain. Other weapon sets provide decent CC with minimal DPS loss.

  1. "On Your Knees!"
  2. Kick
  3. Tremor (swap to mace)
  4. Hushblade (swap to dagger)

Sustain in Open World

  1. Switch to Healing Signet for baseline healing and to maintain Signet Mastery
  2. Use Relic of the Biomancer for baseline healing
  3. Use Relic of the Mistfire for life siphon and additional damage
  4. Use "We Will Never Yield!" in emergencies
  5. Use Dolyak Signet for stability

Latest patch changes

  • Sword has been reworked. The grieving variant has been removed from the guide as sword no longer scales well to power.
  • Dagger has been added as a new CC option. This is useful as dagger offers boon-strip as well.
